title: sampera coriacea
intro: Sampera coriacea, also known as the Indian butter tree or sampera, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, derived from its sap and leaves. The health benefits of sampera coriacea include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as relieving respiratory issues like asthma and bronchitis. The plant is traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies, including decoctions and infusions, which can be taken orally or applied topically to the affected area.
